rationale: The core-0.34 candidate is expected to fail EXACTLY the class-(c) golden/version assertions (the taxonomy §4.2 inventory) and NOTHING else. compare-spike-runs.js --mode classify reads the candidate's `npm test` and `test:browser` vitest --reporter=json output and enforces FOUR layers: (1) the observed failing (file -> count) set equals `node`/`browser` byFile exactly; (2) ASSERTION-LEVEL failure signatures — every failing assertion in an expected-red file must match an allowed {titleSubstring, messageRegex} signature at its exact count, so a failure MOVING to a different assertion inside the same red test (e.g. the staleness test's trace-version/record-size contracts, which precede its engine-version pin) is caught even at constant per-file count; (3) must-stay-green titles never appear in the failing set; (4) POSITIVE presence — the must-pass titles appear with status 'passed' at exact multiplicity (absence-of-failure cannot distinguish passed from renamed/skipped). Schema /2 added layers 2 and 4 plus nodeChromiumRequiredKeys (the exact required cross-env digest set for --mode invariants). Schema /3 (PR #20, fitness policy v2) adds MARKER-VALIDATED signatures: a signature carrying lockMarker (currently 'fitnessVector') requires the failure message to hold exactly one well-formed structured marker (src/sim/lock-markers.js, 'FITNESS_VECTOR_LOCK_MISMATCH expected=<8hex> actual=<8hex>') whose expected= field classify() validates against the authoritative digest in src/sim/population-locks.js AT ADJUDICATION TIME — the mutable production lock digest is deliberately NEVER copied into this file (the pre-/3 inventory hardcoded the then-current v1 digest inside a messageRegex and went stale on the first deliberate re-lock). --mode invariants extracts the cross-env digests from the same marker. See docs/engine-assertion-taxonomy-2026-07.md §4.2 and docs/rapier-034-spike-2026-07.md §4.

node.reporter: npm test (vitest run --reporter=json), Node, deterministic + ordinary flavors, tests/browser/** excluded

tests/evaluation-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion staleness pin (0.19.3 -> 0.19.3-c13133ad.0) + eval-A/B/C/D golden trace digests

allowedFailureSignatures.why: the test checks trace/fixture versions, recordBytes, step counts, dt, and fnv1a consistency BEFORE the engine pin — only the engine-version assertion (its committed custom message) is the allowed failure point
each golden fixture must fail at the checkpoint-divergence formatter (physics moved), never at the structural recordCount/byteCount assertions that precede it
the allowed failure is the checkpoint divergence (or its digest backstop against the committed 5a219735), never the lock-internal-consistency or recordCount/byteCount contracts before it

tests/population-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion pin, fitness-vector digest (committed lock -> measured, via the structured marker), champion solo-trace digest

allowedFailureSignatures.why: 15+ policy/schema/world-mode/seed/dt contracts precede the engine pin in this test — only the engine-version assertion (its committed custom message) may fail

this test's FIRST half is internal repeatability (a===b, MUST hold); the allowed failure is only the second half's fitness-vector golden comparison, which fails with the structured marker (src/sim/lock-markers.js) — classify() additionally parses the marker and requires expected= to equal the authoritative digest in src/sim/population-locks.js at adjudication time, so the mutable lock digest is never copied here. A repeatability failure prints 'expected false to be true' and matches neither the regex nor the marker.
allowed failure is the champion-trace checkpoint divergence (or its digest backstop against the committed f5c5f0c7), never the structural count assertions before it

tests/bench-schema.test.js.asserts: report.meta.rapier == package.json dependency pin { compat: '0.19.3', deterministicCompat: '0.19.3' }

allowedFailureSignatures.why: the meta.rapier toEqual expected side always prints the 'deterministicCompat' key — unique to that assertion in this test regardless of how the diff wraps; the schema/os/cpu/fixture assertions before it never mention it
the rapierVersion .toBe('0.19.3') failure message ends the expected side with the quoted bare pin; the schema/dt/hard-check assertions around it never print that literal

tests/integrity-probe-schema.test.js.asserts: report.engine.rapierVersion pin ('0.19.3') — the integrity probe's engine attestation (added by PR #20's numerical-integrity policy; mirrors the physics-explosion probe pin)

allowedFailureSignatures.why: the smoke test's rapierVersion .toBe('0.19.3') is its only engine pin and precedes nothing engine-sensitive that could red first (schema/passes/policy asserts are pure; the identity/agreement/dt hard checks are engine-neutral and stay green); the file's other tests (single-pass dispatch, unknown-pass, the parametric-jitter discrete-gene regressions) carry no pin and stay green

tests/evolution-determinism.test.js.asserts: evolution artifact runtime identity pin before history/component golden locks

allowedFailureSignatures.why: runtime identity is asserted before every evolution artifact digest, so an engine candidate must red here rather than masquerade as an unexplained history-format drift

tests/evolution-probe-schema.test.js.asserts: evolution identity probe runtime version pin

allowedFailureSignatures.titleSubstring: the report is well-formed, versioned, and every hard check passes
allowedFailureSignatures.why: the explicit runtime-version assertion precedes aggregate hard-lock checks, keeping the candidate red attributable to the engine rather than the probe schema

tests/evolution-replay.test.js.asserts: independent v1 artifact producer/runtime identity pin

allowedFailureSignatures.titleSubstring: an independently produced Kimi artifact resumes and continues byte-identically
allowedFailureSignatures.messageRegex: engine changed — re-lock the independent evolution artifact deliberately
allowedFailureSignatures.why: the fixture and candidate runtime versions are compared before byte equality or replay, so a planned engine spike fails at a stable identity tooth

node.mustPassRationale: The failing-set exclusion (mustPassAssertionSubstrings) plus the POSITIVE presence requirement (mustPassPresent: each title observed with status 'passed' at exact multiplicity — e.g. FOUR passed 'two fresh worlds agree' fixtures) together enforce that the internal-determinism gate-(a) checks and the pure/structural teeth actually RAN and PASSED on the candidate. Every substring was verified unique to a single Node test file, so whole-report counting cannot double-count.

browser.reporter: npm run test:browser (vitest run --config vitest.browser.config.js --reporter=json), pinned Chromium
browser.note: The local historical spike SKIPPED test:browser; these per-file counts + allowedFailureSignatures were FINALIZED from the heavy CI bootstrap dispatch (C5, July 2026, under fitness policy v1) observed failing set, and are re-validated by every subsequent heavy run's candidate browser classify. PR #20 (fitness policy v2) updated the fitness-vector signature to the structured-marker protocol — the message shape was re-verified with a real flipped-lock Chromium capture (2026-07-16) and is re-validated by the next heavy dispatch. The load-bearing cross-env check is independent: --mode invariants requires the COMPLETE nodeChromiumRequiredKeys set to be extracted from BOTH environments with equal values.

tests/browser/evaluation-determinism.tes.asserts: A-D golden trace digests, in Chromium

allowedFailureSignatures.titleSubstring: digest, counts, and every checkpoint state match the golden lock
allowedFailureSignatures.note: Chromium analog of the Node 'run matches the committed lock' / 'to be null' x4: each of the four golden A-D fixtures diverges at the FIRST checkpoint under core 0.34 (expect.fail carries the full '<fixture> [<UA>]: first divergent checkpoint N (state)' message, untruncated). A move to an EARLIER project-contract assertion would not carry this message, so this is the allowed class-(c) golden move, not a regression.

tests/browser/population-determinism.tes.asserts: population fitness-vector + champion-trace digests, in Chromium

allowedFailureSignatures.note: Chromium mirror of the Node marker red: the candidate's measured fitness-vector digest differs from the committed lock, reported via the same structured marker and validated against src/sim/population-locks.js at adjudication time (no digest literal lives here). Historical: the July 2026 C5 run predated fitness policy v2 and failed on the then-current v1 digest 'bded0d30' via Vitest's .toBe formatting — its committed evidence under docs/ is unchanged.
Chromium mirror of the Node 'champion solo digest-mode rerun' / 'to be null' red: the champion trace diverges from the locked trace under core 0.34.

tests/browser/evolution-determinism.test.asserts: browser evolution runtime pin plus independent v1 artifact producer/runtime pin

allowedFailureSignatures.titleSubstring: the committed artifact reproduces exactly in the browser
allowedFailureSignatures.messageRegex: engine changed — re-lock deliberately via the Node gate
allowedFailureSignatures.why: the browser golden gate checks runtime identity before component and history digests

allowedFailureSignatures.titleSubstring: Chromium continues the independent Kimi artifact byte-identically
allowedFailureSignatures.messageRegex: engine changed — re-lock the independent evolution artifact deliberately
allowedFailureSignatures.why: the independent artifact test compares runtime identity before local bytes or replay

browser.mustPassRationale: The Chromium population 'pure initializer locks' test hashes PURE genotype/initializer data (no physics), so it MUST run and pass on the candidate — enforced by presence even while browser per-file counts remain bootstrap (null).

nodeChromiumRequiredKeysRationale: The digest(s) whose cross-env agreement is MECHANICALLY verifiable from vitest failure messages: only population:fitness-vector, which BOTH gates emit via the structured FITNESS_VECTOR_LOCK_MISMATCH marker (src/sim/lock-markers.js — a short custom message on the golden .toBe, untruncated in both reporters; --mode invariants parses `actual=` from it and additionally validates `expected=` against src/sim/population-locks.js). The eval A-D checkpoint states and the champion trace are NOT message-extractable cross-env: Node's `.toBeNull()` on a formatted string is TRUNCATED by the reporter (the state hex is dropped), while Chromium's `expect.fail(fullMessage)` keeps it — an irreconcilable reporter asymmetry surfaced by the first heavy dispatch. Extending the marker protocol to those locks is possible future work; until then dt readback + this one agreeing digest + the full file/count/signature/mustPass classification remain the cross-env determinism evidence.

mustPassInvariants.dtReadback: world.timestep reads back Math.fround(1/60) = 0.01666666753590107 on the candidate (the probe/runner dt tooth stays GREEN — else the whole physics suite cascade-reds for the wrong reason)
mustPassInvariants.internalDeterminismGateA: the determinism gate's 'two fresh worlds agree on digest + every checkpoint' (internal repeatability) PASSES on the candidate for A-D; only gate (d) 'matches committed lock' is allowed to move — enforced by mustPassPresent, not just absence-of-failure
mustPassInvariants.nodeChromiumAgreement: the candidate's MEASURED digests are identical between the Node and Chromium determinism-gate failure printouts for the COMPLETE nodeChromiumRequiredKeys set (cross-env determinism holds on the new engine)

timing.gate: compare-spike-runs.js --mode timing parses the candidate's probe:timing log: every `DRIFT <check name>` line must name a check in allowedDriftChecks (any OTHER drift fails the arm — ten unrelated API-contract drifts must not hide behind one expected exit 1), and the probe's exit code must be consistent with the parsed DRIFT lines (exit 0 with drifts, or exit 1 with none, is a parse/crash inconsistency and fails). An allowlisted drift that VANISHED (probe fully green) is reported loudly as a class-(b) flip-watch, not fatal — the drift is recorded, not a contract.
timing.note: the single class-(b) engine-finding the local run saw (exit 1 on probe:timing, 36 checks / 1 DRIFT, adoption-critical sub-checks green: dt readback, isValid/isSleeping, timing member surface)

heavyEvidence.rationale: The DECLARED coverage of a heavy=true run, enforced by compare()'s arm-usability check on BOTH arms: `--pass prevalence` (defaultConfig, no --smoke) generates the three Phase-1A characterization masters each with 20 unique individuals, and `--prevalence-seeds 20260730` generates the fresh seed with 20. A one-row / missing-seed / missing-fresh-seed prevalence artifact must NOT participate in a citable heavy verdict — the scientific catastrophic COUNTS stay observations, but the experiment's declared coverage (which seeds, how many individuals) is a contract. Derived from scripts/probe-physics-explosion.js smokeConfig/defaultConfig (populationSize 20).

bootstrapCompleteRationale: The TWO-STAGE citability gate (machine-enforced, not narrated). While false, the browser byFile inventory was NOT finalized (null counts, no allowedFailureSignatures), so an unrelated browser-contract regression in an expected-red browser file could not be caught — so compare() forced `citable=false` on EVERY run. STAGE 1 (the heavy=true BOOTSTRAP dispatch, run 29428463562) produced the browser observed failing set; its counts + assertion-level signatures are now committed in the browser section and this flag is flipped true. STAGE 2 (any subsequent heavy=true run against this finalized inventory) is citable: its candidate browser classify re-validates these exact signatures, so a hidden browser-contract regression now fails the arm. `titlesPendingFirstHeavyRun` is the human-readable twin of this flag. Flipping this true REQUIRES every browser byFile entry to carry a non-null integer expectedFailures AND an allowedFailureSignatures array (enforced by the compare-spike-runs self-consistency test).
